title: Ctenopteris rigescens
taxonomy: Ctenopteris rigescens is classified within the plant family Polypodiaceae, which includes over 1,000 species of ferns. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic relationships. The genus Ctenopteris is thought to be endemic to New Zealand. The family Polypodiaceae is part of the larger group Polypodiales, which consists of various fern families. Ctenopteris rigescens is a species of terrestrial fern.
uses: Ctenopteris rigescens is a plant native to New Zealand, and research has shown some potential medicinal uses for its leaves. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used by the Maori people for various health benefits. Studies have suggested that the plant may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use. The leaves are also being investigated for their potential in treating certain skin conditions due to their antimicrobial properties.
